agm_validation.description: The Affected Genomic Model (AGM) specialist confirms which modified organism a finding refers to, using its ID, name, and species.

allele.description: The Allele specialist confirms allele and variant identities against the Alliance database.

controlled_vocabulary_validation.description: The Controlled Vocabulary specialist confirms terms by their list name, term name, abbreviation, and synonym.

data_provider_validation.description: The Member Group specialist confirms group codes and checks that the group and species line up.

experimental_condition_validation.description: The Experimental Condition specialist brings together ontology, controlled-vocabulary, member-group, species, and chemical checks into one decision for each condition.

gene.description: The Gene specialist confirms gene identities and pulls full gene details against the Alliance database.

gene_expression.description: The Gene Expression agent confirms gene names it found while reading the PDF.

gene_ontology.description: The Gene Ontology agent looks up GO terms.

ontology_term_validation.description: The Ontology Term specialist confirms exact ontology IDs and resolves term names or synonyms, keeping note of any cases that stayed unclear.

subject_entity_validation.description: The Subject specialist confirms the affected entity — sending it to the gene, allele, or modified-organism path depending on what it is.

diagnostic.hint: For PDF evidence and document-tool advice, inspect the live runtime catalog with `get_tool_inventory` and `get_tool_details` instead of relying on remembered tool schemas.

curies.description: CURIE list for generic mapping helpers

entity_curies.description: Entity CURIEs for generic entity lookup

entity_names.description: Entity names or labels for generic entity lookup

force.description: Backward-compatible no-op; search methods no longer perform local symbol-shape validation

provider_name.description: Data provider display name for provider lookup

terms.description: CURIE list for bulk ontology term lookup

vocabulary.description: Controlled vocabulary name or display label

agent_studio.prompt_description: Structured lookups in the Alliance Curation Database. Narrow results to a member group: MGI, FB, WB, ZFIN, RGD, SGD, HGNC.

bulk_list_optimization.instruction:
TOOL EFFICIENCY REQUIREMENT:
The request includes a large entity list. Minimize tool rounds.
Prefer bulk lookup methods when available (for example search_genes_bulk/search_alleles_bulk) using list inputs instead of one-call-per-entity loops.
If provider/model supports it, issue parallel tool calls in the same turn.
Avoid one-call-per-entity loops when enough evidence already exists to synthesize output.

agr_curation_query.description: Look things up in the Alliance Curation Database — genes, alleles, ontology terms, controlled-vocabulary terms, species, and member groups — through one tool with many specific lookup methods.

parameters.description: Which lookup to run (for example, search for a gene or fetch an allele by ID). This decides what is looked up.
parameters.name: method

parameters.description: Gene symbol to search for.
parameters.name: gene_symbol

parameters.description: List of gene symbols for bulk lookup.
parameters.name: gene_symbols

parameters.description: A gene ID for a direct lookup.
parameters.name: gene_id

parameters.description: Allele symbol to search for.
parameters.name: allele_symbol

parameters.description: List of allele symbols for bulk lookup.
parameters.name: allele_symbols

parameters.description: An allele ID for a direct lookup.
parameters.name: allele_id

parameters.description: Generic entity helper type, such as gene, allele, or agm.
parameters.name: entity_type

parameters.description: Entity labels or symbols for generic entity-to-CURIE lookup.
parameters.name: entity_names

parameters.description: A list of entity IDs to look up facts for.
parameters.name: entity_curies

parameters.description: Filter by group/provider: MGI, FB, WB, ZFIN, RGD, SGD, HGNC.
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: Data provider display name for provider lookup.
parameters.name: provider_name

parameters.description: Maximum results to return.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: A search word, an ID, or a controlled-vocabulary term to look up.
parameters.name: term

parameters.description: A list of term IDs to look up in bulk.
parameters.name: terms

parameters.description: Controlled vocabulary name or label filter.
parameters.name: vocabulary

parameters.description: Controlled vocabulary term name.
parameters.name: term_name

parameters.description: Controlled vocabulary term abbreviation.
parameters.name: abbreviation

parameters.description: Controlled vocabulary term synonym.
parameters.name: synonym

parameters.description: Which kind of ontology term to limit the lookup to (for example, a GO term or a mouse phenotype term).
parameters.name: ontology_term_type

parameters.description: Category for generic CURIE-to-name mapping.
parameters.name: category

parameters.description: A list of IDs to map to display names.
parameters.name: curies

documentation.summary: A single tool for looking up Alliance curation data. It offers many specific methods — search for a gene, fetch an allele by ID, look up an ontology term, list member groups, and more. Different agents use the methods that fit their job.

get_allele_by_exact_symbol.description: Find the allele whose official symbol exactly matches.
get_allele_by_exact_symbol.name: Get Allele by Exact Symbol

get_allele_by_id.description: Get full details for one allele by its ID.
get_allele_by_id.name: Get Allele by ID

get_data_provider.description: Look up one Alliance member group by its short code, full name, or species ID, and flag any cases where the group and species do not line up.
get_data_provider.name: Get Data Provider

get_data_providers.description: List every Alliance member group, with its short code, species ID, and full name where available.
get_data_providers.name: Get Data Providers

get_gene_by_exact_symbol.description: Find the gene whose official symbol exactly matches.
get_gene_by_exact_symbol.name: Get Gene by Exact Symbol

get_gene_by_id.description: Get full details for one gene by its ID.
get_gene_by_id.name: Get Gene by ID

get_ontology_term.description: Fetch one exact ontology term by its ID, optionally limited to a certain kind of term.
get_ontology_term.name: Get Ontology Term

get_ontology_terms.description: Fetch several exact ontology terms by their IDs at once.
get_ontology_terms.name: Get Ontology Terms

get_vocabulary_term.description: Fetch one exact term from a controlled list by its name, abbreviation, or synonym. You can narrow the search to a named part of the list, or leave that out to search the whole list.
get_vocabulary_term.name: Get Vocabulary Term

map_curies_to_names.description: Turn a list of IDs into their human-readable names.
map_curies_to_names.name: Map CURIEs to Names

map_entity_curies_to_info.description: Get facts about Alliance entities from their IDs.
map_entity_curies_to_info.name: Map Entity CURIEs to Info

map_entity_names_to_curies.description: Turn entity names or labels into their Alliance IDs, for a stated species.
map_entity_names_to_curies.name: Map Entity Names to CURIEs

search_alleles.description: Find alleles whose symbol contains the text you give.
search_alleles.name: Search Alleles

search_alleles_bulk.description: Search for many allele symbols at once in a single call.
search_alleles_bulk.name: Search Alleles (Bulk)

search_anatomy_terms.description: Search anatomy terms for a specific species.
search_anatomy_terms.name: Search Anatomy Terms

search_genes.description: Find genes whose symbol contains the text you give.
search_genes.name: Search Genes

search_genes_bulk.description: Search for many gene symbols at once in a single call.
search_genes_bulk.name: Search Genes (Bulk)

search_go_terms.description: Search Gene Ontology (GO) terms by name or keyword.
search_go_terms.name: Search GO Terms

search_life_stage_terms.description: Search developmental-stage (life-stage) terms for a specific species.
search_life_stage_terms.name: Search Life Stage Terms

search_ontology_terms.description: Search ontology terms by name within one chosen kind of term (for example, mouse phenotype terms).
search_ontology_terms.name: Search Ontology Terms

search_vocabulary_terms.description: Search a controlled list for terms by name, abbreviation, or synonym. You can narrow it to a named part of the list, or search the whole list.
search_vocabulary_terms.name: Search Vocabulary Terms

agr_curation_query.name: AGR Curation Query

required_tool_call.failure_message: did not call required database lookup tools before answering
required_tool_call.guardrail_message: You must query the package-declared curation lookup tool before answering. Use agr_curation_query first.

agr_literature_reference_lookup.description: Look up a paper in the Alliance literature collection by its ID, or find candidate papers by title or citation text.

example_queries: Resolve PMID:27528223 to an Alliance reference
Search Hahm KB Suppressed Helicobocter pylori by title text

parameters.description: Choose whether to fetch one exact paper by ID/title, or search for likely matches.
parameters.name: method

parameters.description: A known ID or exact title for the paper you want — a PubMed ID, DOI, Alliance reference ID, or member-group reference ID.
parameters.name: identifier

parameters.description: A title, citation, or identifier to search for when you do not have an exact match.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: When searching, require an exact match instead of likely matches.
parameters.name: exact_match

parameters.description: The most candidate papers to return from a search.
parameters.name: limit

documentation.summary: Finds papers in the Alliance literature collection. Give it a known ID (such as a PubMed ID or DOI) to fetch the exact paper, or a title/citation to search for likely matches. It reports what it tried, the candidate papers it found, any cases where the match was unclear or nothing matched, and a plain status message.

get_literature_reference.description: Fetch one exact paper by a known identifier — a PubMed ID (PMID), DOI, Alliance reference ID, member-group reference ID, or exact title.
get_literature_reference.name: Get Literature Reference

search_literature_references.description: Search for likely-matching papers by title, citation text, or any known identifier when you do not have an exact match.
search_literature_references.name: Search Literature References

agr_literature_reference_lookup.name: AGR Literature Reference Lookup

agr_species_context_lookup.description: Work out the species, the Alliance group, and the standard species ID for a paper, without looking up any genes or alleles.

parameters.description: A species name from the paper, such as Drosophila melanogaster or C. elegans.
parameters.name: species

parameters.description: An Alliance group code, such as FB, WB, MGI, HGNC, ZFIN, RGD, or SGD.
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: The full display name of the group or organism.
parameters.name: provider_name

parameters.description: A standard NCBI Taxonomy species ID, such as NCBITaxon:7227 (fruit fly).
parameters.name: taxon_id

parameters.description: The most matching groups to return.
parameters.name: limit

documentation.summary: A focused lookup that matches a species name to its Alliance group (such as FlyBase or WormBase) and its standard NCBI Taxonomy ID. It is just for species and group context; it does not look up genes, alleles, or other entities.

agr_species_context_lookup.name: AGR Species Context Lookup

parameters.description: Full URL to query (must be on alliancegenome.org domain).
parameters.name: url

parameters.description: HTTP method (default: GET).
parameters.name: method

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request headers.
parameters.name: headers_json

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request body.
parameters.name: body_json

documentation.summary: Finds orthology relationships — genes that correspond to one another across different species — using Alliance of Genome Resources data.

alliance_api_call.name: Alliance Orthology API

attach_evidence_to_object.description: Link a saved piece of evidence to the finding it supports.

parameters.description: Which saved piece of evidence to link.
parameters.name: evidence_record_id

parameters.description: The finding to link the evidence to.
parameters.name: object_id

parameters.description: The draft finding to link the evidence to.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The specific field this evidence supports, if it applies to just one field.
parameters.name: field_path

documentation.summary: Connects a saved piece of evidence to a finding (or to a specific field of it), without changing the quote itself or where it came from.

attach_evidence_to_object.name: Attach Evidence To Object

chebi_api_call.description: Look up a chemical compound in ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest, a standard chemical database).

parameters.description: Full URL to query (must be on ebi.ac.uk domain).
parameters.name: url

parameters.description: HTTP method (default: GET).
parameters.name: method

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request headers.
parameters.name: headers_json

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request body.
parameters.name: body_json

documentation.summary: Looks up chemical compound IDs and related information in ChEBI, the standard chemical-compound database hosted at EBI.

chebi_api_call.name: ChEBI API

curation_db_sql.description: Run a direct query against the Alliance Curation Database to look up Disease Ontology terms and how they relate to one another.

parameters.description: The database query to run against the curation database.
parameters.name: query

documentation.summary: Runs a direct database query to find Disease Ontology terms (DOID, the standard set of disease term IDs) and the relationships between them.

curation_db_sql.name: Curation Database SQL

detach_evidence_from_object.description: Remove a link between a saved piece of evidence and a finding it was wrongly tied to.

parameters.description: Which saved piece of evidence to unlink.
parameters.name: evidence_record_id

parameters.description: The finding to unlink the evidence from.
parameters.name: object_id

parameters.description: The draft finding to unlink the evidence from.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The specific field link to remove, if it applied to just one field.
parameters.name: field_path

documentation.summary: Disconnects a saved piece of evidence from a finding (or from a specific field) it was linked to. The evidence itself stays saved.

detach_evidence_from_object.name: Detach Evidence From Object

discard_allele_observation.description: Drop one draft allele from the run's working list when it turned out to be wrong or unsupported.

parameters.description: Identifies which draft entry on the working list this action applies to.
parameters.name: candidate_id

parameters.description: A short note explaining why the draft is being dropped, kept in the run's history.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Marks one draft allele as withdrawn so it will not appear in the final result. The entry is set aside rather than erased: it stays in the run's history for auditing, with the short reason the agent gave for dropping it.

discard_allele_observation.name: Discard Allele Observation

discard_disease_observation.description: Drop one draft disease annotation from the run's working list when it turned out to be wrong or unsupported.

parameters.description: Identifies which draft entry on the working list this action applies to.
parameters.name: candidate_id

parameters.description: A short note explaining why the draft is being dropped, kept in the run's history.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Marks one draft disease annotation as withdrawn so it will not appear in the final result. The entry is set aside rather than erased: it stays in the run's history for auditing, with the short reason the agent gave for dropping it.

discard_disease_observation.name: Discard Disease Observation

discard_gene_expression_observation.description: Drop one draft gene-expression observation from the run's working list when it turned out to be wrong or unsupported.

parameters.description: Identifies which draft entry on the working list this action applies to.
parameters.name: candidate_id

parameters.description: A short note explaining why the draft is being dropped, kept in the run's history.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Marks one draft gene-expression observation as withdrawn so it will not appear in the final result. The entry is set aside rather than erased: it stays in the run's history for auditing, with the short reason the agent gave for dropping it.

discard_gene_expression_observation.name: Discard Gene Expression Observation

discard_gene_mention_evidence.description: Drop one draft gene from the run's working list when it turned out to be wrong or unsupported.

parameters.description: Identifies which draft entry on the working list this action applies to.
parameters.name: candidate_id

parameters.description: A short note explaining why the draft is being dropped, kept in the run's history.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Marks one draft gene as withdrawn so it will not appear in the final result. The entry is set aside rather than erased: it stays in the run's history for auditing, with the short reason the agent gave for dropping it.

discard_gene_mention_evidence.name: Discard Gene Mention Evidence

discard_phenotype_observation.description: Drop one draft phenotype annotation from the run's working list when it turned out to be wrong or unsupported.

parameters.description: Identifies which draft entry on the working list this action applies to.
parameters.name: candidate_id

parameters.description: A short note explaining why the draft is being dropped, kept in the run's history.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Marks one draft phenotype annotation as withdrawn so it will not appear in the final result. The entry is set aside rather than erased: it stays in the run's history for auditing, with the short reason the agent gave for dropping it.

discard_phenotype_observation.name: Discard Phenotype Observation

discard_recorded_evidence.description: Withdraw a saved piece of evidence that turned out to be wrong or too weak, while keeping it in the run's history.

parameters.description: Which saved piece of evidence to withdraw.
parameters.name: evidence_record_id

parameters.description: A short note on why the evidence should not be kept.
parameters.name: reason

documentation.summary: Sets one piece of evidence aside so it will not be used in the final result. It is not erased — it stays in the run's history for auditing and is left out of the output by default.

discard_recorded_evidence.name: Discard Recorded Evidence

finalize_allele_extraction.description: Submit the run's draft allele list so the specialists can confirm each one.

parameters.description: The list of draft entries the agent wants to hand off for confirmation.
parameters.name: candidate_ids

documentation.summary: Takes the allele drafts the agent settled on and hands them off for confirmation by the Allele Validation specialist, which confirms the official allele/variant against the Alliance database. The agent proposes the set; the specialists confirm the official identities and produce the records that curators review. Each finished allele draft is handed off as a small bundle of linked records (the allele, the paper, and how they connect). These records are held back from being written or exported on their own until the allele specialist confirms the official allele/variant.

finalize_allele_extraction.name: Finalize Allele Extraction

finalize_disease_extraction.description: Submit the run's draft disease annotation list so the specialists can confirm each one.

parameters.description: The list of draft entries the agent wants to hand off for confirmation.
parameters.name: candidate_ids

documentation.summary: Takes the disease annotation drafts the agent settled on and hands them off for confirmation by the disease specialists, which confirm the disease term, the affected gene/allele/model, evidence codes, and provider against the Alliance database. The agent proposes the set; the specialists confirm the official identities and produce the records that curators review. The handoff turns each draft into the right kind of disease record (gene, allele, or model) based on the affected subject the agent chose.

finalize_disease_extraction.name: Finalize Disease Extraction

finalize_gene_expression_extraction.description: Submit the run's draft gene-expression observation list so the specialists can confirm each one.

parameters.description: The list of draft entries the agent wants to hand off for confirmation.
parameters.name: candidate_ids

documentation.summary: Takes the gene-expression observation drafts the agent settled on and hands them off for confirmation by the gene-expression specialists, which confirm the gene and the controlled terms against the Alliance database. The agent proposes the set; the specialists confirm the official identities and produce the records that curators review.

finalize_gene_expression_extraction.name: Finalize Gene Expression Extraction

finalize_gene_extraction.description: Submit the run's draft gene list so the specialists can confirm each one.

parameters.description: The list of draft entries the agent wants to hand off for confirmation.
parameters.name: candidate_ids

documentation.summary: Takes the gene drafts the agent settled on and hands them off for confirmation by the Gene Validation specialist, which confirms the official gene against the Alliance database. The agent proposes the set; the specialists confirm the official identities and produce the records that curators review.

finalize_gene_extraction.name: Finalize Gene Extraction

finalize_phenotype_extraction.description: Submit the run's draft phenotype annotation list so the specialists can confirm each one.

parameters.description: The list of draft entries the agent wants to hand off for confirmation.
parameters.name: candidate_ids

documentation.summary: Takes the phenotype annotation drafts the agent settled on and hands them off for confirmation by the phenotype specialists, which confirm the affected entity and the phenotype term against the Alliance database. The agent proposes the set; the specialists confirm the official identities and produce the records that curators review.

finalize_phenotype_extraction.name: Finalize Phenotype Extraction

get_agent_contract.description: Look up an agent's setup details — which tools it uses, the shape of its output, which specialists confirm its findings, and the rules on individual fields — without running anything.

parameters.description: Which agent's setup you want to look at.
parameters.name: agent_id

parameters.description: Which part of the setup to show: its tools, its output shape, its data structure, the specialists tied to it, its ontology rules, or one specific field.
parameters.name: topic

parameters.description: The specific field to look at, when you want details on just one field.
parameters.name: field_path

parameters.description: Choose a short overview or the full details.
parameters.name: detail_level

documentation.summary: Shows a read-only summary of how a configured agent is set up: its tools, its output structure, the specialists tied to it, the ontology rules on its fields, and field-by-field details. It only reports the setup; it does not run the agent or change anything.

get_agent_contract.name: Agent Contract Lookup

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The specific fields to correct on the draft, each paired with its new value.
parameters.name: updates

documentation.summary: Updates specific fields on one draft allele the agent staged earlier — for example fixing a typo in a captured value or attaching better supporting quotes. Only the small set of fields meant to be editable can be changed; the rest of the draft stays as recorded.

quickgo_api_call.description: Look up a Gene Ontology term in QuickGO.

parameters.description: Full URL to query (must be on ebi.ac.uk domain).
parameters.name: url

parameters.description: HTTP method (default: GET).
parameters.name: method

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request headers.
parameters.name: headers_json

parameters.description: Optional JSON string for request body.
parameters.name: body_json

documentation.summary: Looks up Gene Ontology (GO) term details — names, definitions, and relationships — from the QuickGO service.

quickgo_api_call.name: QuickGO API

read_chunk.description: Read the full text of one passage from the PDF and get back the exact pieces of it that can be saved as evidence.

parameters.description: Which passage to read, taken from a search result or a section's list of passages.
parameters.name: chunk_id

documentation.summary: Returns the complete text of one passage, pointers to the passages just before and after it, and the specific snippets within it that can be captured as supporting evidence. This is the step where evidence is chosen, since the snippets selected here become the exact verified quote on a saved piece of evidence.

read_chunk.name: Read Chunk

read_section.description: Read the full text of a named section of the paper.

parameters.description: The section to read, such as Methods or Introduction.
parameters.name: section_name

documentation.summary: Returns the complete text of every passage in a section, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order, so it gives full coverage of the section even when a search might overlook a faintly matching passage. This is the way to read an entire section, including tables and figure legends. The individual passages it lists can then be read in full to pick out exact supporting quotes.

read_section.name: Read Section

read_subsection.description: Read the full text of a named subsection inside a section of the paper.

parameters.description: The section that contains the subsection.
parameters.name: section_name

parameters.description: The subsection to read.
parameters.name: subsection_name

documentation.summary: Returns the complete text of every passage in one subsection, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order, so it gives full coverage of that subsection even when a search might overlook a faintly matching passage. This is the way to read a whole subsection, including its figure legends. The relevant passages can then be read in full to pick out exact supporting quotes.

read_subsection.name: Read Subsection

record_evidence.description: Save one piece of supporting evidence from the PDF, using snippets picked out of a passage that was read.

parameters.description: The thing this evidence is about (for example, the gene or allele name).
parameters.name: entity

parameters.description: The snippets to save, picked from a passage that was read.
parameters.name: span_ids

documentation.summary: Turns chosen snippets from a passage into a saved piece of evidence with the exact verified quote and where it came from in the paper. Each call saves one piece of evidence; if several snippets are chosen together, they are stored as one joined quote.

record_evidence.name: Record Evidence

resolve_domain_field_term.description: Settle the official standard term for one field and return a confirmed value with its supporting trail, ready to use.

parameters.description: Which curation area this field belongs to.
parameters.name: domain_pack_id

parameters.description: The kind of record this field is part of.
parameters.name: object_type

parameters.description: Which field is being settled.
parameters.name: field_path

parameters.description: The phrase from the paper that this term is being matched to.
parameters.name: source_phrase

parameters.description: Optional supporting context — quote, page, section, provider, or species.
parameters.name: evidence_context

parameters.description: The ID of the candidate term picked from the search or inspect results.
parameters.name: candidate_curie

parameters.description: A candidate value that is a name rather than an ID, picked from the search results (for example a controlled-list term name).
parameters.name: candidate_value

parameters.description: The Alliance group to scope the lookup to.
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: The species context, kept for the record.
parameters.name: taxon

parameters.description: The most candidates to check while confirming.
parameters.name: limit

documentation.summary: Confirms the one official standard term for a field, after the agent has explored candidates with the search and inspect tools. Its result is the trusted choice the agent can record, with a clear trail of how the term was decided.

resolve_domain_field_term.name: Resolve Domain Field Term

search_document.description: Search the uploaded PDF for passages relevant to a query, using meaning-based matching, exact word matching, or a blend.

parameters.description: What to search the paper for.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: The most matching passages to return (default 5).
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Limit the search to certain sections, such as Methods or Results.
parameters.name: section_keywords

parameters.description: How to match: by meaning (best for broad concepts), by exact words (best for specific symbols and IDs), or a blend. The default blends both.
parameters.name: search_mode

documentation.summary: Finds passages in the uploaded PDF that match a query. The default blended search combines meaning-based and exact-word matching, so it catches passages worded differently from the query (for example "expressed in" versus "detected in"). Exact-word matching is best for specific gene symbols, strains, alleles, probes, or identifiers, and very short queries automatically lean on exact-word matching. The search can be limited to particular sections, such as Results or figure legends. The matching passages can then be read in full to pick out exact supporting quotes.

search_document.name: Search Document

search_domain_field_terms.description: Search for candidate standard terms (from an ontology or controlled list) that could fill one specific field, based on a phrase the agent read in the paper.

parameters.description: Which curation area this field belongs to (for example, gene expression).
parameters.name: domain_pack_id

parameters.description: The kind of record this field is part of.
parameters.name: object_type

parameters.description: Which field the agent is trying to fill (for example, "where expressed").
parameters.name: field_path

parameters.description: The phrase from the paper to look up candidate terms for.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Optional supporting context from the paper — the source phrase, quote, figure, provider, or species.
parameters.name: evidence_context

parameters.description: The Alliance group to scope a species-specific anatomy lookup to.
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: The species context, kept for the record.
parameters.name: taxon

parameters.description: An optional starting point in the ontology to limit the search to one branch.
parameters.name: branch_root_curie

parameters.description: The most candidate terms to return.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Require an exact name match instead of close matches, where supported.
parameters.name: exact_match

documentation.summary: Casts a wide net for possible standard-term matches for one field, starting from a phrase backed by evidence in the paper. The results are suggestions to compare, not a final choice — the agent settles on the official term separately with Resolve Domain Field Term.

search_domain_field_terms.name: Search Domain Field Terms

stage_allele_observation.description: Add one new draft allele to the run's working list, backed by the exact wording the agent found in the paper.

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The exact wording for this entity as it appears in the paper.
parameters.name: mention

parameters.description: The supporting quotes (captured earlier from the PDF) that back up this draft.
parameters.name: evidence_record_ids

parameters.description: The exact paper phrases the agent read this finding from.
parameters.name: source_mentions

parameters.description: A cleaned-up version of the allele name the agent suggests, offered as a hint for the allele specialist.
parameters.name: normalized_hint

parameters.description: The gene this allele belongs to, if the paper names it.
parameters.name: associated_gene

parameters.description: The species this allele belongs to, if stated.
parameters.name: taxon

parameters.description: The title of the paper the allele was read from.
parameters.name: reference_title

parameters.description: The file name of the uploaded paper the allele was read from.
parameters.name: reference_filename

documentation.summary: Records a single allele the agent read in the paper, together with the supporting quotes it captured — a first-pass draft, not a final record. Afterward, the Allele Validation specialist confirms the official allele or variant against the Alliance database and settles its identity. One call adds one draft entry.

stage_allele_observation.name: Stage Allele Observation

stage_disease_observation.description: Add one new draft disease annotation to the run's working list, backed by the exact wording the agent found in the paper.

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The exact wording for this entity as it appears in the paper.
parameters.name: mention

parameters.description: The disease name as the agent read it from the paper.
parameters.name: disease_name

parameters.description: How the disease appears in this paper: the main disease studied (primary), mentioned as background, used for comparison (comparative), the disease being modeled (model context), or unspecified. This is the agent's read of the paper context, not a stored relationship.
parameters.name: role

parameters.description: How sure the agent is about this finding (high, medium, or low).
parameters.name: confidence

parameters.description: The Alliance group this belongs to, such as FB (FlyBase), WB (WormBase), MGI (Mouse), ZFIN (Zebrafish), RGD (Rat), SGD (Yeast), or HGNC (Human).
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: The supporting quotes (captured earlier from the PDF) that back up this draft.
parameters.name: evidence_record_ids

parameters.description: The exact paper phrases the agent read this finding from.
parameters.name: source_mentions

parameters.description: The Disease Ontology ID (DOID, a standard disease term ID) the agent proposes, if it found one; the disease specialist confirms it.
parameters.name: disease_curie

parameters.description: What kind of thing has the disease association — a gene, an allele, or a whole modified organism (model). This choice decides what kind of record is produced.
parameters.name: subject_type

parameters.description: The ID of the gene, allele, or model the disease is associated with.
parameters.name: subject_identifier

parameters.description: The name of the gene, allele, or model the disease is associated with.
parameters.name: subject_label

parameters.description: The official relationship between the subject (gene, allele, or model) and the disease, chosen from the Disease Relation vocabulary — for example "is model of" or "is implicated in". The specialist confirms this term.
parameters.name: disease_relation_name

parameters.description: The Evidence and Conclusion Ontology codes (ECO, a standard set of "how was this shown" codes) the agent proposes for this association.
parameters.name: evidence_code_curies

parameters.description: The genetic sex stated for this finding, if any (a standard controlled value).
parameters.name: genetic_sex_name

parameters.description: Standard qualifier terms that refine the disease association, if the paper states any.
parameters.name: disease_qualifier_names

parameters.description: IDs of additional genes the paper says were involved together with the subject.
parameters.name: with_gene_identifiers

parameters.description: Any experimental conditions the paper explicitly tied to this finding (for example a drug treatment or temperature), with the kind of relationship each had to the result. Left empty when the paper states no conditions.
parameters.name: condition_relations

parameters.description: True when the paper explicitly says the finding did NOT occur.
parameters.name: negated

documentation.summary: Records a single disease annotation the agent read in the paper, together with the supporting quotes it captured — a first-pass draft, not a final record. Afterward, the disease specialists confirm the disease term, the affected gene/allele/model, the evidence codes, and the data provider against the Alliance database and settle the official identity. One call adds one draft entry. It is the richest of the five: one draft carries the disease mention and disease term, the affected subject (gene, allele, or model), evidence codes, the relation (such as "is model of"), the data provider, and any experimental conditions. The choice of subject decides whether the final record is a gene, allele, or model disease annotation.

stage_disease_observation.name: Stage Disease Observation

stage_gene_expression_observation.description: Add one new draft gene-expression observation to the run's working list, backed by the exact wording the agent found in the paper.

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The supporting quotes (captured earlier from the PDF) that back up this draft.
parameters.name: evidence_record_ids

parameters.description: A plain sentence describing where (and when) the gene was seen to be expressed, in the agent's words from the paper.
parameters.name: where_expressed_statement

parameters.description: The gene whose expression was observed, with the exact phrase from the paper and the agent's proposed symbol/ID.
parameters.name: subject

parameters.description: The paper the observation came from, with the exact phrase and its reference ID.
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: The standard-term choices the agent made for this observation (such as the anatomy and developmental-stage terms), each paired with the value it selected. A term specialist confirms these.
parameters.name: controlled_fields

parameters.description: Any experimental conditions the paper explicitly tied to this finding (for example a drug treatment or temperature), with the kind of relationship each had to the result. Left empty when the paper states no conditions.
parameters.name: condition_relations

documentation.summary: Records a single gene-expression observation the agent read in the paper, together with the supporting quotes it captured — a first-pass draft, not a final record. Afterward, the gene-expression specialists confirm the gene and the controlled terms against the Alliance database and settle the official identity. One call adds one draft entry. One draft carries the gene whose expression was observed, the reference it came from, a plain "where expressed" statement, and the controlled-term choices (such as the anatomy and developmental-stage terms) that a term specialist confirms.

stage_gene_expression_observation.name: Stage Gene Expression Observation

stage_gene_mention_evidence.description: Add one new draft gene to the run's working list, backed by the exact wording the agent found in the paper.

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The exact wording for this entity as it appears in the paper.
parameters.name: mention

parameters.description: The supporting quotes (captured earlier from the PDF) that back up this draft.
parameters.name: evidence_record_ids

parameters.description: The agent's notes on how it figured out which gene this mention refers to.
parameters.name: identity_resolution_notes

parameters.description: How sure the agent is about this finding (high, medium, or low).
parameters.name: confidence

parameters.description: The species named in the paper for this gene, if stated.
parameters.name: species

parameters.description: The NCBI Taxonomy ID (a standard species ID, e.g. NCBITaxon:7227 for fruit fly) the agent suggests, if it could tell.
parameters.name: taxon_hint

parameters.description: The Alliance group the agent thinks this gene belongs to, as a hint for the specialist.
parameters.name: data_provider_hint

parameters.description: The gene ID the agent proposes, offered as a hint; the gene specialist confirms the official one.
parameters.name: proposed_primary_external_id

parameters.description: The gene symbol the agent proposes, offered as a hint.
parameters.name: proposed_gene_symbol

parameters.description: The species ID the agent proposes for this gene, offered as a hint.
parameters.name: proposed_taxon

documentation.summary: Records a single gene the agent read in the paper, together with the supporting quotes it captured — a first-pass draft, not a final record. Afterward, the Gene Validation specialist confirms the official gene against the Alliance database and settles its identity. One call adds one draft entry. The agent records the mention plus its best identity hints; settling the official gene is left to the specialist.

stage_gene_mention_evidence.name: Stage Gene Mention Evidence

stage_phenotype_observation.description: Add one new draft phenotype annotation to the run's working list, backed by the exact wording the agent found in the paper.

parameters.description: A short label the agent gives this draft so it can refer back to the same entry later in the run.
parameters.name: pending_ref_id

parameters.description: The plain-language description of the phenotype as stated in the paper.
parameters.name: phenotype_annotation_object

parameters.description: The supporting quotes (captured earlier from the PDF) that back up this draft.
parameters.name: evidence_record_ids

parameters.description: The exact paper phrases the agent read this finding from.
parameters.name: source_mentions

parameters.description: The ID of the gene, allele, or model that shows this phenotype.
parameters.name: subject_identifier

parameters.description: The name of the gene, allele, or model that shows this phenotype.
parameters.name: subject_label

parameters.description: What kind of thing shows the phenotype — a gene, an allele, or a whole modified organism (model).
parameters.name: subject_type

parameters.description: The species ID of the affected entity, if stated.
parameters.name: subject_taxon

parameters.description: The phenotype term ID the agent proposes, if it found one; a term specialist confirms it.
parameters.name: term_curie

parameters.description: The phenotype term name the agent proposes.
parameters.name: term_label

parameters.description: The Alliance group this belongs to, such as FB (FlyBase), WB (WormBase), MGI (Mouse), ZFIN (Zebrafish), RGD (Rat), SGD (Yeast), or HGNC (Human).
parameters.name: data_provider

parameters.description: The species ID tied to the proposed phenotype term, if relevant.
parameters.name: term_taxon_id

parameters.description: Any experimental conditions the paper explicitly tied to this finding (for example a drug treatment or temperature), with the kind of relationship each had to the result. Left empty when the paper states no conditions.
parameters.name: condition_relations

parameters.description: True when the paper explicitly says the finding did NOT occur.
parameters.name: negated

documentation.summary: Records a single phenotype annotation the agent read in the paper, together with the supporting quotes it captured — a first-pass draft, not a final record. Afterward, the phenotype specialists confirm the affected entity and the phenotype term against the Alliance database and settle the official identity. One call adds one draft entry.

stage_phenotype_observation.name: Stage Phenotype Observation

update_recorded_evidence_metadata.description: Update the editable notes on a saved piece of evidence.

parameters.description: Which saved piece of evidence to update.
parameters.name: evidence_record_id

parameters.description: An updated label for what this evidence is about.
parameters.name: entity

parameters.description: The field this evidence supports.
parameters.name: field_path

parameters.description: A note on how this evidence should be used.
parameters.name: agent_note

documentation.summary: Changes the editable details on a piece of evidence — what it is about, which field it supports, and the agent's note. The quote itself and where it came from cannot be changed.

update_recorded_evidence_metadata.name: Update Evidence Metadata
